title: Assess Monte Carlo convergence and error
description: Track the running estimate and its standard error to decide when enough samples have been drawn.
body: Use when running a Monte Carlo estimate to a target precision. Do not stop at a fixed sample count without checking the error, since variance determines how many samples are truly needed. Actions: accumulate the running mean and variance, compute the standard error, and continue until the confidence interval meets the target. Validation signals include a standard error shrinking with the square root of sample count and the estimate stabilizing within the interval. Risk boundary: stopping early reports a falsely precise answer, so escalate sample count or variance reduction when the interval is still too wide.
